relationship_to_restored_text: Interpretive contemplative layer aligned by verse. This does not replace the source-audited restored translation.

verses.mystical_translation: Saul moves from hidden suspicion to an explicit death command, while Jonathan's delight resists him.
Jonathan believes warning is part of love and gives David a place to hide.
He places himself beside the ruler to test what power intends.
Jonathan speaks good in a court organized around accusation.
He names innocent blood and reminds Saul of joy once shared in deliverance.
Saul swears life, but an oath is only as safe as the conduct that follows.
Jonathan restores David to the court on the strength of the promise.
War returns, and success again exposes David to royal fear.
Distress, music, and a spear meet in the house once more.
The weapon reaches the wall while David reaches the night.
State violence surrounds the home, and Michal names the deadline clearly.
She opens a path through the window and chooses life over royal obedience.
A household image occupies the bed where the fugitive cannot remain.
Michal uses illness as a shield against armed messengers.
Saul demands even the bed be carried to his killing presence.
The messengers uncover an absence concealed by domestic objects.
Under threat, Michal offers the explanation most likely to keep herself alive.
David carries the whole pattern of abuse to Samuel and seeks sanctuary.
Surveillance follows him into the prophetic refuge.
The arrest party is interrupted by an experience it cannot command.
Wave after wave of enforcement loses its assigned purpose.
Saul enters the pursuit personally when delegated violence fails.
The same uncontrollable current takes hold of the king.
Royal clothing and control fall away; exposure halts the hunt without making humiliation holy.

contemplative_note: The spiritual movement in this chapter is escape from organized death. Jonathan warns, Michal creates an exit, David tells the story, and sanctuary interrupts the arrest machinery. The prophetic scenes do not make altered states a spectacle; they expose how little control a violent king truly possesses. Safety may require secrecy, distance, and refusal of family authority.
